Ayahs

وَٱلذَّٰرِيَـٰتِ ذَرْوًا (۱)
By the scattering [winds] that scatter [the clouds]; (1)
فَٱلْحَـٰمِلَـٰتِ وِقْرًا (۲)
by the [rain] bearing [clouds] laden [with water]; (2)
فَٱلْجَـٰرِيَـٰتِ يُسْرًا (۳)
by [the ships] which move gently [on the sea]; (3)
فَٱلْمُقَسِّمَـٰتِ أَمْرًا (٤)
by [the angels] who dispense [livelihood] by [His] command: (4)
إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَصَادِقٌ (٥)
indeed what you are promised is true, (5)
وَإِنَّ ٱلدِّينَ لَوَٰقِعٌ (٦)
and indeed the retribution will surely come to pass! (6)

Key Points

Dhāriyāt (ذاریات) is the plural of Dhāriyah (ذاریة), meaning strong winds, derived from dharw (ذرو), meaning to scatter and disperse. Wiqr (وِقر) means a heavy load, and here it refers to rain-laden clouds. Jāriyāt (جاریات) means ships or flowing rivers. Based on narrations (Riwāyāt), the meaning of Dhāriyāt is the winds, the meaning of Hāmilāt (حاملات) is the clouds, the meaning of Jāriyāt is the ships, and the meaning of Muqassimāt (مقسّمات) is the angels who distribute provisions. (1) Yusr (یُسر) is the opposite of ‘Usr (عُسر) and means ease. The meaning of «فالجاریات یُسراً» is the ships that move easily and calmly.

Allah has sworn by the winds, which are the agents of movement for ships, and the clouds, which are the source of blessing for the earth, in these verses. The life of humans, animals, and plants depends on rain, and winds play a crucial role in moving clouds, moderating the weather, and pollinating plants. The role of ships in the past and present life of humanity is undeniable. The widest, cheapest, and least hazardous routes are the sea routes and travel by ship. Their role becomes clear when the world's ships stop moving for just a few days.

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The meaning of «المقسّمات امراً» is the angels who distribute the provisions of the people every day between the break of dawn and sunrise. (2)

In the distribution of provisions, each individual, tribe, and region is given a share of one thing and not another, so that the issues of participation, cooperation, competition, trade, movement, and innovation arise to compensate for the lack, ensuring that no individual or tribe considers itself self-sufficient in every respect and thus resorts to rebellion. Yes, hardships are wisely placed alongside ease. «قرن بسعتها عقابیل فاقتها» (3)

Paying attention to Allah's display of power in the wind, cloud, and ship makes accepting the Resurrection (Ma'ad) easier for man.

At the beginning of this Surah, the certainty of the occurrence of the Day of Judgment is stated through four consecutive oaths. The particles «انّما» and «انّ», the letter lām in the words «لصادق» and «لواقع», and the nominal sentence structure all indicate emphasis.

Examples of Divine promises that have been fulfilled in this world: Allah commanded the mother of Moses to cast her newborn into the water and told her: We will surely return him to you and make him one of the Messengers, «انّا رادوه الیك و جاعلوه من المرسلین» (4). This great promise was fulfilled. Allah promised the Prophet of Islam that He would suffice him against the mockers, «انّا كفیناك المستهزئین» (5). He promised to raise high his mention, «و رفعنا لك ذكرك» (6), and He fulfilled His promises.

Allah has repeatedly promised the victory of truth over falsehood and the good end for the pious, and He has fulfilled His promise. The name of Imam Husayn عليه السلام remains, while the names of his killers have been erased.

Those who fail to keep their promises are either incapable, deceitful, or forgetful, and the Holy Essence of Allah is far removed from all these flaws.

1) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 2) Man Lā Yahḍuruhu al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, p. 504. 3) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 91. 4) Al-Qasas, 7. 5) Al-Hijr, 95. 6) Al-Sharh, 4.

Messages

  1. Swearing by natural phenomena such as cloud, wind, and rain shows Islam's attention to discovering the laws of existence and the governance ruling over them, leading to the recognition of Allah. «و الذاریات... فالحاملات... فالجاریات...»
  2. Allah carries out human affairs through angels. «والمقسّمات امراً»
  3. The Quran directs human thought toward both the tangible and the intangible to elevate human intellect. «والذاریات... والمقسّمات امراً»
  4. In the face of others' doubts, the truth must be spoken with decisiveness, firmness, and clarity. «انما توعدون لصادق» (The word inna and the letter lām are for emphasis.)
  5. The promise of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the end of the world has been continuous and successive. «توعدون» (You are promised/warned)
  6. The God who has displayed such power in creation is not incapable of fulfilling His word. «انما توعدون لصادق»
  7. All these natural and supernatural means (wind, cloud, ship, and angel) are not merely for eating, dying, and perishing. «و انّ الدین لواقع»
  8. Allah fulfills both the promises He made regarding this world and the promises He made regarding the Hereafter. «انما توعدون لصادق و ان الدین لواقع»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Adh-Dhariyat

This Surah has sixty verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of this Surah is taken from its first verse: «والذّاریات ذرواً», meaning the strong winds that scatter things.

The beginning and end of this Surah deal with the Resurrection (Ma'ad). The majority of the verses cover discussions about Monotheism (Tawhid) and the signs of Allah in creation, the story of the angels who were guests of Prophet Abraham عليه السلام and gave him the good news of a son, the warning regarding the destruction of the people of Lot, and a reference to the stories of some Prophets, such as Moses, Noah, and the people of 'Ad and Thamud.

By recounting the history of past Prophets and their hardships and struggles in guiding people, the Quran invites the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله to patience and steadfastness.

Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءِ ذَاتِ ٱلْحُبُكِ (۷)
By the sky full of adornment [with stars], (7)
إِنَّكُمْ لَفِى قَوْلٍ مُّخْتَلِفٍ (۸)
indeed you are of different opinions! (8)
يُؤْفَكُ عَنْهُ مَنْ أُفِكَ (۹)
He who has been turned away [from the truth] is turned away from it. (9)
قُتِلَ ٱلْخَرَّٰصُونَ (۱٠)
Perish the liars, (10)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى غَمْرَةٍ سَاهُونَ (۱۱)
who are heedless in a stupor! (11)

Key Points

Hubuk (حُبُك), the plural of habīkah (حبیكه) and hibāk (حباك), has three meanings: path, adornment, and equilibrium (moderation/balance). For each of these meanings, there is evidence in the Quran describing the sky (heaven): «لقد خلقنا فوقكم سَبع طرائق»(7) We created seven paths above you. «زیّنّا السماء الدنیا بزینة الكواكب»(8) We adorned the lower heaven with the adornment of the stars. «والسّماء رفعها و وضع المیزان»(9) He raised the heaven and established the balance.

Yu'fak (یؤفك) derives from ifk (اِفك), meaning to turn away or lead astray (to cause deviation). Therefore, a lie (ifk) is called ifk because it is a deviation from the truth and a source of deviation.

Kharrāṣ (خرّاص), derived from kharṣ (خَرص), refers to speech that lacks a scientific basis and relies on conjecture, estimation, and guesswork.

Ghamrah (غمرة) refers to abundant water whose depth is unknown. Its meaning here is the profound ignorance and unawareness that engulfs a person.

The work of some people is rumor-mongering based on guesswork, delusion, imposing their fabrications onto others, and creating doubt in them. Usually, the enemy commissions the rumor, the hypocrites produce it, and the simple-minded accept it.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام regarding «یؤفك عنه مَن افك» said: Whoever deviates from and disobeys the Wilayah (guardianship) of the Prophet's Household علیهم السلام will be kept away from Paradise.(10)

7) Mu'minun, 17. 8) Saffat, 6. 9) Ar-Rahman, 7. 10) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 422.

Messages

1- Whoever lacks logic has no stability and speaks differently every moment. Thus, the straight path is one, but the wrong paths are many. The true belief is one, but the false ones are many. «انّكم لفى قول مختلف»

2- The opponents spoke various things about the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, such as: poet, liar, student of others, sorcerer, bewitched, madman, one who seeks help from others, repeater of the thoughts and legends of the ancients, and the like. «انّكم لفى قول مختلف»

3- Every deviation, slip, and sin paves the way for other deviations, slips, and sins. «یؤفك عنه من افك»

4- Speaking without proof or basis, and relying on guesswork and conjecture, is the ground for receiving divine curse. «قتل الخراصون»

5- Cursing and the slogan "Death to..." is a Quranic slogan. «قتل الخرّاصون»

6- The Islamic society must decisively reject (expel) the unworthy individuals. «قتل الخرّاصون»

7- Guesswork, conjecture, and error sometimes occur for individuals, but the danger lies where a person's occupation is guessing and their life is immersed in error and heedlessness. «الخرّاصون... فى غمرة ساهون...» (A person's reliance must be on knowledge and science, not guesswork and conjecture.)

8- One who is immersed in heedlessness and error and lives in a world of conjecture and imagination has no value in life. «قتل الخرّاصون الّذین هم فى غمرة ساهون»

Ayahs

يَسْـَٔلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَوْمُ ٱلدِّينِ (۱۲)
They ask, ‘When will be the Day of Retribution?’ (12)
يَوْمَ هُمْ عَلَى ٱلنَّارِ يُفْتَنُونَ (۱۳)
It is the day when they will be tormented in the Fire, (13)
ذُوقُوا۟ فِتْنَتَكُمْ هَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (۱٤)
[and will be told]: ‘Taste your torment. This is what you used to hasten.’ (14)

Key Points

The word fitnah (فتنه) originally means melting gold and silver in fire to separate the pure from its impurities, and here it means the severity of the punishment (Azaab) of the disbelievers. The deniers used to say to the prophets: «فأتنا بما تعدنا»(11) (Bring us the punishment you promised us). Here, the Quran states: This is the very punishment that you impatiently requested.

Characteristics of the misguided inhabitants of Hell: They contradict themselves, «فى قول مختلف» (in conflicting speech); they turn away from the truth, «یؤفك عنه من افك» (he who is turned away is turned away from it); they are immersed in heedlessness and error, «فى غمرة ساهون» (immersed in heedlessness, being forgetful); they base their intellectual system on conjecture, «الخرّاصون» (the conjecturers); they hold baseless assumptions and inappropriate questions, and they rush the fulfillment of Divine promises. «یسئلون ایّان یوم الدین» (They ask, "When is the Day of Judgment?").

11) Al-A'raf, 70 and Hud, 32.

Messages

1- Asking in order to understand is permissible, but if it is for the sake of finding excuses and denying the truth, it is subject to reproach. «یسئلون»

2- Not knowing the details is not a reason to reject the fundamentals. Some groups refuse to accept the principle of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) because they do not know the exact time of the Hour. «یسئلون ایّان یوم الدین»

3- Those who seek to deny the truths must be dealt with decisively. «یوم هم على النار یفتنون»

4- The punishment of the Day of Judgment is the making of man himself. «ذوقوا فتنتكم»

5- The disbelievers are physically punished by the fire, and spiritually punished by the address «ذوقوا» (Taste). «النار - ذوقوا»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (۱٥)
Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and springs, (15)
ءَاخِذِينَ مَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَبْلَ ذَٰلِكَ مُحْسِنِينَ (۱٦)
receiving what their Lord has given them, for they had been virtuous aforetime. (16)
كَانُوا۟ قَلِيلًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مَا يَهْجَعُونَ (۱۷)
They used to sleep a little during the night, (17)
وَبِٱلْأَسْحَارِ هُمْ يَسْتَغْفِرُونَ (۱۸)
and at dawns they would plead for forgiveness, (18)
وَفِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ حَقٌّ لِّلسَّآئِلِ وَٱلْمَحْرُومِ (۱۹)
and there was a share in their wealth for the beggar and the deprived. (19)

Key Points

Yahja'ūn (یهجعون) is derived from hujū' (هجوع), meaning sleep at night. Ayah 17 is interpreted in two ways: One is that the pious (muttaqīn) are awake for most of the night and sleep for only a small part of it. The other is that the pious rise for worship on most nights, and it is rare for them to be entirely asleep and not rise for worship. Just as in a narration, when Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about "كانوا قلیلاً من الّیل ما یهجعون" (They used to sleep but little of the night), He said: Few are the nights when they happen to sleep and do not have a rising (for worship) during it. (12)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The best time for supplication (du'a) is the time of dawn (sahar), because Allah has said: "و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون" (And in the hours before dawn they would ask forgiveness). (13)

Regarding "و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون," Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent is asking for forgiveness (istighfār) seventy times during the Witr prayer of the Night Prayer (Salāt al-Layl). (14)

Imam Baqir and Imam Sadiq علیهما السلام said: The meaning of the deprived (mahrūm) in the Ayah is one who has no mental defect but whose provision (rizq) has not been expanded. "لیس بعقله بأس و لم یبسط له فى الرزق" (There is nothing wrong with his intellect, but provision has not been expanded for him). (15)

Apparently, this hadith is a response to those who imagine that their ample provision is due to their knowledge, planning, cleverness, expertise, and intellect. Therefore, the Imam said: There are intelligent people whose lives still do not turn smoothly despite their efforts and endeavors.

12) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 446. 13) Mustadrak, Vol. 5, p. 200. 14) Tahdhīb, Vol. 2, p. 130. 15) Kāfī, Vol. 3, p. 500.

Messages

1- For the guidance and instruction of people, the elements of encouragement and threat must be interconnected. "ذوقوا فتنتكم... انّ المتقین فى جنّات..." (Taste your trial... Indeed, the righteous will be in Gardens...)

2- For the pious, there is not just one garden and spring, but magnificent gardens and springs. "جناتٍ و عیونٍ" (The tanwīn [nunation] indicates magnificence).

3- Turning away from fleeting and transient pleasures brings eternal fulfillment. "انّ المتقین فى جنّات... آخذین..."

4- In addition to the springs and gardens, the inhabitants of Paradise receive a fresh blessing from their Lord at every moment. "آخذین ما آتاهم ربّهم" (Receiving what their Lord has given them).

5- What is bestowed upon the inhabitants of Paradise is worthy of the Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) of Allah. "آتاهم ربّهم" (Their Lord has given them).

6- The sacred Essence of Allah Himself gives the gifts of Paradise to the pious. "آتاهم ربّهم" (Similar to Ayah 21 of Sūrat Al-Insān, where He says: "سقاهم ربّهم" – Allah gives them drink).

7- Whoever does not strive in this world will not attain the eternal treasure of the Hereafter. "انهم كانوا قبل ذلك محسنین" (Indeed, they were before that doers of good). Yes, the harvest of the Hereafter is the result of the worldly cultivation.

8- The prerequisite of piety (taqwā) is benevolence (ihsān) and good deeds. "المتّقین... محسنین"

9- The Divine reward is commensurate with human action. The reward for doing good to others today results in receiving goodness tomorrow. "آخذین... محسنین"

10- Good deeds must be the permanent practice (sīrah) of a person. "كانوا... محسنین" (They used to be doers of good).

11- Vigilance during the night is among the signs of the pious. "قلیلاً من الّیل ما یهجعون"

12- Prayer and supplication at night and seeking forgiveness at dawn are the continuous practices of the pious. "یهجعون, یستغفرون" (The imperfect verb form indicates continuity and permanence).

13- All human beings, even the pious, are exposed to error and need forgiveness. «المتقین... هم یستغفرون» (The presence of the word hum [they] means that the people of piety, despite having piety, still constantly seek forgiveness).

14- In the Night Vigil (Tahajjud) and rising before dawn, seeking forgiveness (istighfār) holds a special place among all forms of remembrance (adhkār) and supplications. "و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون"

15- Seeking forgiveness at dawn is the peak of worship. "و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون"

16- Islam is a comprehensive school of thought, placing worship at night alongside service to the deprived. "و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون و فى اموالهم..."

17- A group of people are deprived but do not ask; attention must be paid to them as well. "للسائل والمحروم" (For the petitioner and the deprived).

18- The help you give to the petitioner and the deprived is giving them their own right, because Allah has placed a right for them in the wealth of the people, so do not hold it as a favor over them. "حق للسائل و المحروم" (A right for the petitioner and the deprived).

19- One should not wait for the deprived to state their request before taking action; the dignity and honor of individuals must be preserved. "و فى اموالهم حق... المحروم"

20- Piety does not necessitate poverty and destitution; one can be a wealthy pious person. This is because the Quran states: In their wealth, there is a right for the deprived. Therefore, they must have enough income and wealth to help others in addition to meeting their own life needs. "المتقین... فى اموالهم حق للسّائل و المحروم"

Ayahs

وَفِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ءَايَـٰتٌ لِّلْمُوقِنِينَ (۲٠)
In the earth are signs for those who have conviction, (20)
وَفِىٓ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ أَفَلَا تُبْصِرُونَ (۲۱)
and in your souls [as well]. Will you not then perceive? (21)
وَفِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ رِزْقُكُمْ وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ (۲۲)
And in the sky is your provision and what you are promised. (22)
فَوَرَبِّ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ مِّثْلَ مَآ أَنَّكُمْ تَنطِقُونَ (۲۳)
By the Lord of the sky and the earth, it is indeed the truth, just as [it is a fact that] you speak. (23)

Key Points

The Quran considers the prerequisite for being guided and attaining certainty through the signs (Ayat) of nature and the Book of Sharia (Divine Law) to be a prepared heart: «آیات للموقنین» (Signs for those who are certain), «لقوم یسمعون» (For a people who listen) (16), «لقوم یتفكّرون» (For a people who reflect) (17), «لقوم یعقلون» (For a people who reason) (18), «لكل صبّار شكور» (For every patient and grateful person) (19), «لقوم یؤمنون» (For a people who believe) (20), «لاولى النُهى» (For those possessing intellect) (21), «لآیات للمتوسّمین» (Signs for those who perceive) (22), «لآیات للعالِمین» (Signs for those who know) (23), and «هدىً للمتّقین» (A guidance for the righteous) (24).

Many points are hidden in the creation of the earth and man, including: the volume of the earth, its area and depth, mountains and valleys, its distance from the sun and other planets, the earth's various movements, visible and invisible minerals, vegetation, stored waters, colors, rocks, soils, fruits, plants, the earth's purifying function, its provision of all the needs of its inhabitants, and its possession of nutritional materials for the sustenance of all animals and humans—all of which are signs of Allah's power and wisdom for those seeking certainty.

The various stages of the body, from the sperm-drop (nutfa) to subsequent phases, are signs of His power. The diverse instincts of man are signs of His wisdom and His knowledge of human needs.

The harmony among the body's senses and their lack of conflict with one another, the skeletal and nervous systems, the respiratory system, the digestive system, the visual and auditory systems, the defense system—all of these are signs of the Almighty Allah.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: Allah created you hearing and seeing; you become both happy and angry; you feel both hunger and satiety (25).

Due to weak faith in the predestination of sustenance (Rizq), people fall into sin, theft, shortchanging (under-measuring), greed, stinginess, and various types of misconduct.

The descent of sustenance is based on precise calculation and measure: «عندنا خزائنه و ما ننزّله الاّ بقدر معلوم» (And with Us are the treasures thereof, and We do not send it down except in a known measure) (26). The origin and source are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure.

The Ayah «فى السماء رزقكم و ما توعدون» (And in the heaven is your provision and whatever you are promised) can be interpreted in several ways: A. It refers to rain that descends from the sky, as well as light, clouds, wind, etc. B. It means that the decree of the affairs of the earth's inhabitants is in the heavens. C. The eternal sustenance, which is Paradise, is in the heavens. D. The fulfillment of Divine promises (the descent of mercy or punishment) comes from the direction of the sky (27).

«مثل ما انكم تنطقون» (Just as you speak) means that just as your speech is tangible and real to you, so too are the Divine promises; therefore, do not doubt them.

16) Yunus, 67. 17) Ra‘d, 3. 18) Ra‘d, 4. 19) Ibrahim, 5. 20) Nahl, 79. 21) Ta Ha, 128. 22) Hijr, 75. 23) Rum, 22. 24) Baqarah, 2. 25) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 26) Hijr, 21. 27) Tafsir Rahnamā.

Messages

  1. The signs of Divine power and wisdom on earth become clearer day by day, alongside the advancement of science and the discovery of nature's secrets. «و فى الارض آیات...»

  2. The Divine signs and tokens on earth are numerous and significant. «و فى الارض آیات» (The word «آیات» is plural and indefinite, indicating multiplicity and greatness.)

  3. Knowledge of God must be based on certainty and insight. «و فى الارض آیات للموقنین»

  4. Unless the spirit of belief exists in man, blessings are not counted as signs of Allah. «آیات للموقنین»

  5. Studying nature is a way to strengthen faith and monotheistic belief. «آیات للموقنین»

  6. The existence of man is so magnificent that Allah has placed it on par with the Earth. «فى الارض... فى أنفسكم»

  7. Self-knowledge is the path to God-knowledge. «آیات للموقنین... فى أنفسكم»

  8. The fundamental problem of the disbelievers and the irreligious is their superficiality, and Allah reproaches them for this. «افلا تبصرون» (Insight (Basirah) means a deep and precise look.)

  9. A gaze that is not accompanied by insight is worthy of reproach and blame. «أفلا تبصرون»

  10. The source of sustenance (Rizq) is in the heaven (rain, light, wind, clouds, air, etc.). «و فى السّماء رزقكم»

  11. Every kind of Divine goodness and blessing, or wrath and punishment, descends from the heaven as a reward or penalty. «فى السّماء... ما توعدون»

  12. Heaven and earth are under Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) and management, moving toward perfection. «فوربّ السّماء و الارض»

  13. The provision and decreeing of the sustenance of the earth's inhabitants in the heavens is a manifestation of Allah's expansive Lordship over the heaven and the earth. «فوربّ السّماء و الارض»

  14. The power of speech and articulation is one of the special gifts of the Lord to man, which Allah uses as an example of human speech to affirm the truthfulness of His own word and promise. «انه لحقّ مثل ما انكم تنطقون»

Ayahs

هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيْفِ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلْمُكْرَمِينَ (۲٤)
Did you receive the story of Abraham’s honoured guests? (24)
إِذْ دَخَلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًا ۖ قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌ قَوْمٌ مُّنكَرُونَ (۲٥)
When they entered into his presence, they said, ‘Peace!’ ‘Peace!’ He answered, ‘[You are] an unfamiliar folk.’ (25)
فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰٓ أَهْلِهِۦ فَجَآءَ بِعِجْلٍ سَمِينٍ (۲٦)
Then he retired to his family and brought a fat [roasted] calf, (26)
فَقَرَّبَهُۥٓ إِلَيْهِمْ قَالَ أَلَا تَأْكُلُونَ (۲۷)
and put it near them. He said, ‘Will you not eat?’ (27)

Key Points

"Rāgha" (راغ) means that a person leaves the presence of those present in such a way that they do not understand why he is going. A group of angels entered the house of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) to give the glad tidings of the birth of a son. However, he did not recognize them, but nonetheless invited them into the house and prepared the means of hospitality. But when he saw that they were not eating, he became worried and asked the reason, and they introduced themselves.

In some instances, angels have appeared in human form, examples of which are mentioned in the Quran, such as: the descent of Gabriel upon Lady Maryam (عليه السلام) (28), the presence of Hārūt and Mārūt in the kingdom of Solomon (29), and the descent of angels upon Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) (30).

The Etiquette of Hospitality From these verses and the verses that follow, some etiquette of hospitality can be derived, which we point out: 1. The guest is respected. «ضیف... المكرمین» (Guest... the honored ones) 2. When the guest arrives, he should offer salutations (Salam). «قالوا سلاماً» (They said, "Salāmā") 3. Responding to his salutation is obligatory. «قال سلام» (He said, "Salām") 4. The host should go to prepare food away from the sight of the guest. «فراغ» (Then he slipped away) 5. We should prepare the food in the house and with the help of the family. «الى أهله» (To his family) 6. Hospitality should be swift. «فجاء» (Then he came) (The letter 'Fā' indicates speed and a short time) 7. The best foods should be provided. «بعجل سمین» (With a fat calf) 8. We should not take the guest to the table, but rather bring the table to the guest. «فقرّبه الیهم» (Then he brought it near to them) 9. We should not ask the guest if he has eaten or not. «فراغ الى أهله فجاء» (Prophet Abraham went to his family and prepared food without asking) 10. We should personally serve the guest. «فجاء» (He himself brought the food) 11. The food should be accessible to the guest. «فقرّبه الیهم» (Then he brought it near to them) 12. The guest should accept and eat the food, so that the host does not worry. «ألا تأكلون» (Will you not eat?) 13. First hospitality, then conversation. «فجاء بعجل سمین...» (Then he brought a fat calf...) Afterwards they asked: «فما خطبكم أیّها المرسلون» (Then what is your mission, O messengers?) 14. If the guest has bitter and sweet news, he should mention the sweet news first, then the bitter. «بشروه... ارسلنا الى قوم مجرمین» (They gave him glad tidings... We have been sent to a criminal people) (First the good news of a son, then the descent of punishment upon the criminals)

(28) Maryam, 17. (29) Baqarah, 102. (30) Dhāriyāt, 25.

Messages

  1. The story of Abraham and his guests is worthy of hearing and learning. «هل اتاك»
  2. In guidance and instruction, we should use both the method of analogy and the method of storytelling. «مثل انكم تنطقون هل اتاك حدیث...»
  3. Hospitality is a value, and honoring the guest is the Abrahamic tradition. «ضیف ابراهیم المكرمین»
  4. In offering salutations, all those who enter should offer salutations, not just some. «فقالوا سلاماً»
  5. Offering salutations must be swift and immediately after meeting. «فقالوا» (The letter 'Fā' indicates speed and a short time)
  6. Beginning speech with salutations is a heavenly etiquette, even among the angels. «فقالوا سلاما»
  7. We should respond to the salutations of others, even if they are strangers. «قال سلام قوم منكرون»
  8. The guest is honored, even if he is a stranger. «ضیف... المكرمین... قوم منكرون»
  9. The knowledge of Prophet Abraham was limited. «قوم منكرون» (He did not recognize the guests)
  10. We should also offer hospitality to unknown guests. «قوم منكرون فراغ الى اهله...»
  11. The kitchen should not be exposed to the view of the guest. «فراغ الى أهله فجاء...»
  12. Men and women should participate in preparing food and hosting guests. «فراغ الى أهله فجاء»
  13. The host must be sensitive regarding the hospitality offered to the guest and his condition. «فقرّبه الیهم قال ألا تأكلون»
  14. An angel, even if He appears in human form, does not eat food. «قال ألاتأكلون»

Ayahs

فَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَا تَخَفْ ۖ وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلَـٰمٍ عَلِيمٍ (۲۸)
Then he felt a fear of them. They said, ‘Do not be afraid!’ and they gave him the good news of a wise son. (28)
فَأَقْبَلَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ فِى صَرَّةٍ فَصَكَّتْ وَجْهَهَا وَقَالَتْ عَجُوزٌ عَقِيمٌ (۲۹)
Then his wife came forward crying [with joy]. She beat her face, and said, ‘A barren old woman!’ (29)
قَالُوا۟ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۳٠)
They said, ‘So has your Lord said. Indeed He is the All-wise, the All-knowing.’ (30)

Key Points

«أوجس» (awjasa) derives from «ایجاس» (eejas), meaning the internal feeling or sensing of something. «خیفة» (kheefah) derives from «خوف» (khawf), meaning fear. Its tanwin (nunation) indicates variation and specifies the type, meaning a certain kind of fear overcame Abraham. «صرّة» (ṣarrah) here means wailing and shouting. «صكّ» (ṣakk) means a hard strike or slap, «عجوز» (ʿajūz) means incapable and old, and «عقیم» (ʿaqīm) means barren. Sarah, the first wife of Prophet Abraham, was barren, which is why Abraham married Hagar. However, Allah gave this woman the glad tidings of a child in her old age. Since accepting such a matter was difficult for her, she wailed and cried out, saying, "I was barren in my youth; now that I am old and naturally have no possibility of having a child, how can I have one?!" These verses relate to Prophet Abraham having a child through Sarah, and the intended meaning of «غلام علیم» (a knowing boy) is Isaac. Of course, in Ayah 101 of Surah As-Saffat, the glad tidings of Ishmael's birth through Hagar are mentioned using the expression «غلام حلیم» (a forbearing boy). The fear that is criticized is the fear of the enemy when confronting disbelief (kufr); otherwise, natural fear and anxiety that unconsciously arise for any human being are not a fault for the Prophets.

Messages

1- The host must pay attention to the pleasant and unpleasant dispositions and behaviors of the guest. «فاوجس منهم خیفة»

2- The Prophets were human beings and, like other people, sometimes experienced fears and anxieties. «فاوجس منهم خیفة» (Not eating food is a sign of, so to speak, not being indebted [or bound by hospitality] and a type of enmity.)

3- Every behavior or action, no matter how simple or insignificant (like not eating food), if it causes anxiety to others, must be compensated for in some way so that the anxiety is removed. «ألا تأكلون - فاوجس منهم خیفة - لاتخف و بشّروه»

4- The perfection of a child lies in knowledge and learning. «بغلام علیم»

5- The Prophets were sometimes informed of the future through angels. «بشّروه بغلام علیم»

6- Woman is an emotional and sentimental being, and she usually reacts very quickly to what she hears. «فى صرّة فصكّت»

7- A man should not prevent the natural expression of a woman's emotions. «فى صرّة فصكّت» (Prophet Abraham did not prevent his wife from expressing her emotions.)

8- The power of Allah is limitless. He brings forth a talented child from an old man and a barren old woman. «غلام علیم... عجوز عقیم»

9- The Will of Allah governs natural factors. «قالوا كذلك...»

10- All of Allah's bestowals and withholdings are based on knowledge and wisdom. «انه هو الحكیم العلیم» (Indeed, He is the Wise, the Knowing.) (Allah does not give Abraham and his wife a child in their youth, but in their old age, He gives them a son who becomes a Prophet.)

Ayahs

۞ قَالَ فَمَا خَطْبُكُمْ أَيُّهَا ٱلْمُرْسَلُونَ (۳۱)
He said, ‘O messengers, what is now your errand?’ (31)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّآ أُرْسِلْنَآ إِلَىٰ قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ (۳۲)
They said, ‘We have been sent toward a guilty people, (32)
لِنُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِّن طِينٍ (۳۳)
that We may rain upon them stones of clay, (33)
مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۳٤)
sent for the transgressors from your Lord. (34)
فَأَخْرَجْنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۳٥)
So We picked out those who were in it of the faithful, (35)
فَمَا وَجَدْنَا فِيهَا غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۳٦)
but We did not find there other than one house of Muslims, (36)
وَتَرَكْنَا فِيهَآ ءَايَةً لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ (۳۷)
and We have left therein a sign for those who fear a painful punishment.’ (37)

Key Points

Khathb (خَطب) refers to an important matter that a person seeks to accomplish, and musawwamah (مسوّمة) means marked or branded. Prophet Lot (عليه السلام) was a relative of Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) and was engaged in preaching to the people in the region of Sodom in Jordan during Abraham's time. His people were given to immorality (fahsha) and sodomy (liwāṭ), and they did not heed the warnings of Prophet Lot (عليه السلام). Allah overturned their region. The story of this people is also mentioned in Surahs Al-A'raf, Hud, Al-Hijr, and Al-'Ankabut. Hierarchy must be observed. Prophet Abraham (عليه السلام) was the Prophet of firm resolve (Ulul-'Azm) of his time. Therefore, regarding the destruction of Lot's people, the angels first came into the presence of Abraham, stated their mission, and then proceeded to Prophet Lot (عليه السلام). Divine wrath and punishment, delivered by a shower of stones (ḥijāratun min ṭīn), descended upon the Companions of the Elephant (Aṣḥāb al-Fīl) in addition to the people of Lot. "تَرْمِيهِم بِحِجَارَةٍ مِّن سِجِّيلٍ" (31) The Companions of the Elephant intended evil against the sanctity of the House of Allah (the Ka'bah), and the people of Lot intended evil against the sanctity of the house of prophethood (Prophet Lot) and sought to assault his guests; both were destroyed. To aid Prophet Lot (عليه السلام), the angels came with marked stones, and to aid the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) in the Battle of Badr, marked angels came. "يُمْدِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ بِخَمْسَةِ آلَافٍ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ مُسَوِّمِينَ" (32) Muslim is not only the name for the community (Ummah) of the Prophet of Islam, but it is applied to anyone who submits to Allah and follows the heavenly religions. "مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ"

31) Al-Fīl, 4. 32) Āl 'Imrān, 125.

Messages

1- Asking questions is not a fault; even the Prophets asked about what they did not know. "مَا خَطْبُكُمْ..."

2- The punishment of criminals is not only in the Hereafter. "أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَى قَوْمٍ مُّجْرِمِينَ"

3- Sometimes the agents of punishing criminals in this world are also angels. "لِنُرْسِلَ عَلَيْهِمْ حِجَارَةً مِّن طِينٍ"

4- In Divine wrath or mercy, everything has a measure and a mark. "مُسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ"

5- Faith is the key to salvation. "فَأَخْرَجْنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ"

6- If there is only one Muslim family in a region, attention must be paid to them. "غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ"

7- Although a corrupt society provides the ground for corruption, it does not compel a person to be corrupt, and it is possible to have a sound family in a corrupt region. "غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ"

8- Sometimes corruption becomes so widespread that a noble Prophet like Lot (عليه السلام) has only one supporting family, "غَيْرَ بَيْتٍ مِّنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ," and not even the entire family, but the family without the wife. "فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ" (33)

9- Both Divine blessings and Divine wrath are signs (āyah). "وَتَرَكْنَا فِيهَا آيَةً"

10- Those who take heed from bitter events are those who fear the punishment of the Day of Judgment. "آيَةً لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ الْعَذَابَ"

11- Constant fear of Divine punishment is a virtue. "يَخَافُونَ الْعَذَابَ الْأَلِيمَ" 33) Al-A'rāf, 83.

Ayahs

وَفِى مُوسَىٰٓ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳۸)
And in Moses [too there is a sign] when We sent him to Pharaoh with a manifest authority. (38)
فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكْنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سَـٰحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ (۳۹)
But he turned away assured of his might, and said, ‘A magician or a crazy man!’ (39)
فَأَخَذْنَـٰهُ وَجُنُودَهُۥ فَنَبَذْنَـٰهُمْ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٌ (٤٠)
So We seized him and his hosts, and cast them into the sea, while he was blameworthy. (40)

Key Points

The meaning of "سلطان مبین" (Sultan Mubin - Clear Authority/Proof) is a clear reason and proof by which one can argue with others and present one's case through logical means. "نبذ" (Nabd) means the throwing away of insignificant and trivial things. "ملیم" (Malīm), derived from "لوم" (Lawm) and "ملامت" (Malāmat - blame/reproach), refers to someone who deserves censure. The intent of "رُكنه" (Ruknah - support/pillar) is Pharaoh's power, army, wealth, history (or prestige), and propaganda. That is, Pharaoh relied on his power to fight Moses and refused to accept his invitation.

Messages

1- There are many lessons in the history of the prophets. "و فى موسى"

2- To reform society, one must go to the source (of corruption). "أرسلناه الى فرعون"

3- The weapon of the prophets against opponents is strong logic and a clear miracle. "سلطان مبین"

4- Military, economic, and propaganda pillars cause the tyrants and oppressors (Taghuts) to show arrogance and disregard for the call of truth. "فتولّى بركنه"

5- Deviants try to silence those who seek truth using force and power, and introduce them as misguided, sorcerers, or madmen. "ساحرٌ او مجنون"

6- Divine wrath follows the completion of the argument (proof). "سلطان مبین... قال ساحر... فاخذناه..."

7- The army should not blindly obey everyone. Divine wrath also encompassed Pharaoh's armies. "فاخذناه و جنوده"

8- Tyrants (Taghuts) and all their forces are very weak and insignificant before Divine wrath. "فنبذناهم"

9- Deviants suffer both apparent destruction and internal reproach. "اخذناه... و هو ملیم"

10- The wrath of Allah encompasses all deviants, but corrupt leaders deserve special censure. "و هو ملیم" (while he was blameworthy) was used, not: "و هم ملیمون" (and they were blameworthy).

Ayahs

وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ (٤۱)
And in ‘Ad when We unleashed upon them a barren wind. (41)
مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَىْءٍ أَتَتْ عَلَيْهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتْهُ كَٱلرَّمِيمِ (٤۲)
It left nothing that it came upon without making it like decayed bones. (42)

Key Points

The Prophet of the people of 'Ad was Hud (عليه السلام). No matter how much he warned his people, they paid no attention to his words and thus prepared the ground for the descent of the punishment (Azaab). Winds are sometimes generative and factors of growth: «ارسلنا الریاح لواقح» (34), «یرسل الریاح مبشرات» (35), and sometimes they are barren (sterile) and a means of destruction and ruin. «الریح العقیم» means the destructive wind. The wind that was the agent of punishment for the people of 'Ad was so scorching and poisonous that it destroyed humans, animals, plants, and everything that lay in its path. Ramīm (رمیم) means decay or rottenness, and tarmīm (ترمیم) means the repair of decay. Although the people of 'Ad were very powerful and tall, the wind that blew from Divine wrath crushed and shattered them like rotten bones.

34) حجر، 22. 35) روم، 46.

Messages

1- Natural phenomena, such as wind and rain, and their positive and negative effects, are in the hands of Allah. «ارسلنا علیهم الرّیح العقیم»

2- Natural calamities, such as drought and famine, can be Divine punishment. «ما تذر من شى ء... جعلته كالرّمیم»

3- Water and wind are the armies of Allah against the oppressors. «نبذناهم فى الیمّ... الریح العقیم»

Ayahs

وَفِى ثَمُودَ إِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ حِينٍ (٤۳)
And in Thamud, when they were told, ‘Enjoy for a while.’ (43)
فَعَتَوْا۟ عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ ٱلصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ (٤٤)
Then they defied the command of their Lord; so the thunderbolt seized them as they looked on. (44)
فَمَا ٱسْتَطَـٰعُوا۟ مِن قِيَامٍ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُنتَصِرِينَ (٤٥)
So they were neither able to rise up, nor to come to one another’s aid. (45)
وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا فَـٰسِقِينَ (٤٦)
And the people of Noah aforetime. Indeed, they were a transgressing lot. (46)

Key Points

The Prophet of the people of Thamud was Hazrat Salih (عليه السلام), who, at the request of his people, brought forth a she-camel from the heart of the mountain and commanded them not to harm it. However, they killed the she-camel and denied Salih. The meaning of «حتّى حین» (until a time) is the three-day respite that was granted to them so that perhaps they would regret and repent. But they did not abandon their sins, and after three days, the Divine punishment descended. As He states in Ayah 65 of Surah Hud: «تمتّعوا فى داركم ثلاثة أیّام ذلك وعد غیر مكذوب»

Messages

1- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to grant respite to the criminals. «تمتّعوا حتّى حین»

2- Divine commands arise from His Lordship (Rububiyyah) and are for the training of humanity. «أمر ربّهم»

3- Transgression of Divine limits is the cause of destruction. «فعتوا... فاخذتهم»

4- Water, wind, and thunderbolts can become instruments of Allah’s wrath. «الیمّ... الریح... الصاعقة»

5- Witnessing the descent of punishment while being unable to flee indicates the peak of the criminals' humiliation. «ینظرون... فما استطاعوا...»

6- Those who do not learn lessons from the past become a lesson for others. «و قوم نوح من قبل...» (The people of 'Ad did not learn lessons from the people of Noah, who preceded them.)

7- The history of the people of Noah, who did not believe even after a thousand years of preaching by Hazrat Noah (عليه السلام), is a source of lesson for the Prophet and the Muslims. «و قوم نوح من قبل...»

8- Worse than transgression (Fisq) is persistence in transgression. «كانوا... فاسقین»

9- Sometimes sin becomes widespread and general, paving the way for the descent of punishment. «قوماً فاسقین»

Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءَ بَنَيْنَـٰهَا بِأَيْي۟دٍ وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ (٤۷)
We have built the heaven with might, and indeed it is We who are its expanders. (47)
وَٱلْأَرْضَ فَرَشْنَـٰهَا فَنِعْمَ ٱلْمَـٰهِدُونَ (٤۸)
And We have spread out the earth, so how excellent spreaders We have been! (48)
وَمِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ خَلَقْنَا زَوْجَيْنِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ (٤۹)
In all things We have created pairs so that you may take admonition. (49)

Key Points

The word ayd can be the plural of yad, meaning hand, similar to the Ayah: «أم لهم أید یبطشون بها» (36). It can also be a verbal noun (masdar), meaning strength and power. Just as various words derived from this root meaning confirmation (ta'yīd) have appeared in the Quran, such as: «هو الّذى أیّدك بنصره و بالمؤمنین» (37). The words farsh and mahd mean spreading something out for rest.

36) Al-A'raf, 195. 37) Al-Anfal, 62.

Messages

1- The heaven is expanding, and this expansion continues. «انّا لموسعون»

2- The law of pairing (zawjiyyah) governs everything. «من كلّ شى ء... زوجین» (Fertility and the continuation of life exist because of pairing.)

3- The presentation of natural phenomena in the Quran is for the purpose of instruction and removing heedlessness. «لعلّكم تذكّرون»

Ayahs

فَفِرُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٥٠)
‘So flee toward Allah. Indeed, I am from Him a manifest warner to you. (50)
وَلَا تَجْعَلُوا۟ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ ۖ إِنِّى لَكُم مِّنْهُ نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ (٥۱)
Do not set up another god besides Allah. Indeed I am from Him a manifest warner to you.’ (51)

Key Points

In these two verses, the phrase nadhīr mubīn (clear warner) has appeared twice consecutively. In other verses, expressions such as «قرآن مبین» (clear Qur’an) (38), «بلاغ مبین» (clear message) (39), and «عربىّ مبین» (clear Arabic) (40) have also appeared, all of which indicate the clarity and illuminating nature of Allah's message and speech, and the warnings of the Prophets. The result of fleeing towards Allah is fleeing from darkness to light, from ignorance to knowledge, from anxiety to assurance, from superstitions to truth, from dispersion to unity, from polytheism (shirk) to monotheism (tawhīd), and from sin to piety and God-fearingness (taqwā). Imam Baqir عليه السلام considered the journey of Hajj (pilgrimage) to be one of the manifestations of fleeing towards Allah. (41)

38) Hijr, 1. 39) Nahl, 82. 40) Shu‘arā’, 195. 41) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 256.

Messages

1- Limited man, facing thousands of material and spiritual issues, and caught between true and false desires presented to him by himself or others, needs a strong refuge. The best refuge is Allah. «ففرّوا الى اللّه»

2- Man has no choice but to move and flee towards Allah. «ففرّوا الى اللّه»

3- Reflection upon existence must lead man to turn his attention to Allah and worship Him. «ففرّوا الى اللّه», (Considering the preceding verses)

4- The path of the Prophets is clear and transparent. «نذیر مبین»

5- Seeking refuge in strangers and other than Allah is ineffective. «و لاتجعلوا مع اللّه...»

6- The Prophets are commissioned to warn and caution the people. «انى لكم منه نذیر»

Ayahs

كَذَٰلِكَ مَآ أَتَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُوا۟ سَاحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌ (٥۲)
So it was that there did not come any apostle to those who were before them but they said, ‘A magician,’ or ‘A crazy man!’ (52)
أَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِهِۦ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ (٥۳)
Did they enjoin this upon one another?! Rather, they were a rebellious lot. (53)
فَتَوَلَّ عَنْهُمْ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِمَلُومٍ (٥٤)
So turn away from them, as you will not be blameworthy. (54)
وَذَكِّرْ فَإِنَّ ٱلذِّكْرَىٰ تَنفَعُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٥٥)
And admonish, for admonition indeed benefits the faithful. (55)

Key Points

Ayah 52 is like a conclusion of the previous verses and serves as consolation for the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: if the polytheists of Mecca treat you in this manner, know that they treated the prophets before you in the same way. Since the Prophet did not fall short in inviting the people and completed Allah's proof (Hujjah) upon them, the Prophet's turning away from them and abandoning argumentation with them will not result in blame or reproach. These verses, alongside the command to turn away from the rebellious disbelievers, recommend giving admonition (Tadhakkur) to the believers, so that it is not assumed that only disbelievers need warning and admonition. «فتولّ... و ذكّر» There is an effect in hearing that is not present in mere knowing. The passion, sincerity, and method of expression of the speaker can leave a superior impact on a person. Therefore, the Prophet is commanded to continuously admonish the believers.

Messages

  1. All prophets had opponents and were subjected to accusations. Therefore, do not worry about the accusations of opponents. «كذلك... قالوا ساحر أو مجنون»

  2. Contrary to the Quran, which introduces the prophets as human beings just like other humans, «قل انّما أنا بشر مثلكم»(42) opponents tried to attribute the deeds and words of the prophets to their connection with the Jinn. «ساحر أو مجنون»

  3. The consistency in the behavior and speech of the opponents of the prophets throughout history is such that it is as if they all recommended to one another the stance of opposing the truth and leveling accusations of magic and madness. «أتواصَوا...»

  4. The root of the accusations against the prophets is rebellion (Tughyan). «قوم طاغون»

  5. A person has no responsibility towards obstinate individuals. «فتولّ»

  6. After the completion of the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah), turning away from those who fight the truth is permissible. «فتولّ عنهم فماانت بملوم»

  7. Abandoning and leaving others is not always a bad thing. «فتولّ عنهم فما انت بملوم»

  8. The preacher must know the disposition of the audience: turn away from one group, «فتول», and be an admonisher to another group. «و ذكّر»

  9. If we lack the spirit of accepting advice, we should doubt our faith, because a believer accepts advice. «فانّ الذكرى تنفع المؤمنین»

  10. If faith exists, a person accepts admonition from anyone. «فانّ الذكرى تنفع المؤمنین» and He did not say: «فانّ تذكّرك»

  11. Religious preachers should not become weary, for their work is beneficial. «فانّ الذكرى تنفع...»

  12. Believers also need admonition. «فانّ الذكرى تنفع المؤمنین»

  13. The teachings of the heavenly religions are rooted in human nature (Fitrah). The duty of the prophets is to awaken and remind humanity of these innate matters. «ذكّر فانّ الذكرى تنفع المؤمنین» (42) Kahf, 110.

Ayahs

وَمَا خَلَقْتُ ٱلْجِنَّ وَٱلْإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ (٥٦)
I did not create the jinn and the humans except that they may worship Me. (56)
مَآ أُرِيدُ مِنْهُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ وَمَآ أُرِيدُ أَن يُطْعِمُونِ (٥۷)
I desire no provision from them, nor do I desire that they should feed Me. (57)
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلرَّزَّاقُ ذُو ٱلْقُوَّةِ ٱلْمَتِينُ (٥۸)
Indeed it is Allah who is the All-provider, Powerful and All-strong. (58)

Key Points

In a hadith from Imam Husayn عليه السلام, we read: Allah did not create people except for gnosis (ma'rifah) and recognition, and when they recognize Him, they worship Him, and whoever worships Him becomes independent of servitude to others. (43) The Jinn were created before humans and bear responsibility (taklif). In this Ayah, their name also precedes that of humans. «و ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس» In the Quran, all creation is based on truth (Haqq), and no creature is vain (batil). «و ما خلقنا السموات و الارض و ما بینهما الاّ بالحق» (44) And all creatures are for the benefit of humanity. «خلق لكم» (45), «سخّر لكم» (46), «متاعاً لكم» (47) And man, too, was created for a sublime goal, the path to which is the worship of God. «و ما خلقت الجن والانس الا لیعبدون» Of course, worship ('ibadah) has a general meaning, and any good deed performed with a divine intention is called worship.

Question: Other goals, such as trial and testing, have been mentioned in the Quran for the creation of man. Are those goals consistent with the goal mentioned in this Ayah (56)?

Answer: In the second Ayah of Surah Mulk, the Quran states: «خلق الموت والحیاة لیبلوكم أیّكم أحسن عملا» He created death and life to test you [to see] which of you is best in deed. And in the twelfth Ayah of Surah Talaq, it states: «لتعلموا انّ اللّه على كلّ شى ء قدیر» The goal of creation is man's knowledge of Divine power. In summarizing these Ayat, we conclude that the primary goal is servitude to Allah, but worship requires gnosis (ma'rifah), which is expressed in the phrase «لتعلموا», and in worship—which is the acceptance of the Divine path and the abandonment of non-Divine paths—man is tested, which is mentioned in the phrase «لیبكوكم».

Question: If the goal of human creation is worship, then why has this goal not been fully realized?

Answer: Allah has stated repeatedly in the Quran that if He had willed, He would have guided everyone by compulsion and brought them into His servitude. However, Allah created humanity free, so that they may worship Him consciously and freely.

Question: Is Allah in need of our worship?

Answer: The Quran states repeatedly: Allah is Self-Sufficient (Ghani) in every respect; He is independent not only of man but of all existence. If we are instructed to build our house facing the sun, no one should imagine that the sun needs our house; rather, it is we who are in need of its light and energy. In these Ayat, the Self-Sufficiency of Allah is also stated: «ما أرید منهم من رزق»

Question: If Allah is the Provider (Razzaq), why does He not give everyone equal provision (rizq)?

Answer: Being the Provider (Razzaq) is one of Allah's attributes; another of His attributes is being the Wise (Hakim). Equal provision is not a wise matter; rather, it must be dependent on the effort and striving of people. The Quran states: «فامشوا فى مناكبها» (48) Ascend the slopes of the earth and attain provision. «وابتغوا من فضل اللّه» (49) Go forth seeking livelihood and Divine grace, and trade. Just as we should not expect a teacher to give all students the same grade, because in addition to being a teacher and having the authority to give grades, he is also wise (Hakim) and must differentiate between diligent and playful students.

Question: If Allah is the Provider (Razzaq), what are famines for?

Answer: Sometimes shortages have a punitive aspect, as the Quran states regarding the punishment of the Children of Israel: «فبظلم من الّذین هادوا حرّمنا» (50) Because of the injustice committed by the Jews, We forbade certain foods to them. Sometimes they have a testing aspect: «و لنبلونّكم بشى ء من الخوف و الجوع» (51) We test you through hunger, fear, and so on. Sometimes they have an educational aspect: «فاخذناهم بالبأساء... لعلّهم یتضرّعون» (52) We chastise them through hardships so that they may humble themselves and return to Allah. And sometimes they are the inevitable result of man's improper interaction with nature, such as the destruction of forests, the pollution of river water, and the disruption of the ecological balance, which over time leads to many problems, including famine. If we believe that provision (rizq) comes from Allah, the roots of much greed, stinginess, deceit, envy, fraud, and short-changing will dry up within man. «انّ اللّه هو الرّزاق...»

43) Tafsir-e Nemuneh. 44) Hijr, 85. 45) Baqarah, 29. 46) Jathiyah, 13. 47) Nazi'at, 33. 48) Mulk, 15. 49) Jumu'ah, 10. 50) Nisa', 160. 51) Baqarah, 155. 52) An'am, 42.

Messages

1- Jinn and humans share the same responsibility (taklif). «الجنّ و الانس... لیعبدون»

2- The worship performed by humanity and the people becoming divine is important, not that Allah should be worshipped. «لیعبدون» (He did not say: «لأعبد» so that I may be worshipped.)

3- The prerequisite for worship is the gnosis (ma'rifah) of the Worshipped One; thus, the creation of Jinn and humans is for the recognition of the Truth (Haqq) and movement on the path of Allah. «لیعبدون»

4- Man is exposed to heedlessness (ghaflah) and forgetfulness, and his goal must be constantly reminded to him. «و ذكّر... ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس...»

5- Allah is Self-Sufficient, and the worship He demands from Jinn and humans is not due to His need but for their growth. «و ما أرید منهم من رزق...»

6- He who is Himself the Provider (Razzaq), what need does He have for the provision of His servants? «ما أرید منهم من رزق... ان اللّه هو الرّزاق»

7- All facilities, expertise, and tools are means and contexts for attaining provision, but the Provider is Allah alone. «انّ اللّه هو الرّزاق»

8- Allah has no deficiency in His ability to provide. «هو الرّزاق ذوالقوّة»

9- The power of Allah to provide does not diminish with the passage of time. «المتین» (Al-Matin means firm and steadfast.)

Ayahs

فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذَنُوبًا مِّثْلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصْحَـٰبِهِمْ فَلَا يَسْتَعْجِلُونِ (٥۹)
Indeed the lot of those who do wrong [now] will be like the lot of their [earlier] counterparts. So let them not ask Me to hasten on [that fate]. (59)
فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن يَوْمِهِمُ ٱلَّذِى يُوعَدُونَ (٦٠)
Woe to the faithless for the day they are promised! (60)

Key Points

Dhanoob (ذَنوب) here means share or portion. (53) The Sūrah began with tūʿadūn (توعدون) and ends with yūʿadūn (یوعدون), indicating that the purpose of this Sūrah was to explain the issue of the Resurrection (Ma'ad). These verses state: The oppressors of this nation also have a share of the punishment, similar to the share of their counterparts in other nations who were destroyed. So why are they rushing, saying: Bring our punishment sooner! (54)

In the culture of the Quran, polytheism (shirk) and disbelief (kufr) are considered the greatest oppression (ẓulm): oppression against Allah, when His creation is placed beside Him or in His stead; oppression against oneself, when one turns to the creation instead of the Creator; and oppression against the family, who are deprived of recognizing the truth and following it.

Allamah Tabataba'i states: The meaning of ẓulm (oppression) in li-lladhīna ẓalamū (للّذین ظلموا) in verse 59, considering li-lladhīna kafarū (للّذین كفروا) in verse 60, is the oppression of disbelief (kufr), not oppression against people.

Although it has been said that the intent of yawmahumu alladhī yūʿadūn (یومهم الّذى یوعدون) might be the Day of the Battle of Badr (worldly punishment), the strong probability is that it refers to the Day of Resurrection (Hereafter's punishment). (55)

53) The reason yastaʿjilūn (یستعجلون) has a kasrah (vowel sound 'i') is that it was originally yastaʿjilūnī (یستعجلونى), and the kasrah indicates the omission of the letter yā (ى). 54) Tafsir al-Mizan. 55) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Whoever does not worship Allah and does not place himself on the path of the purpose for which he was created is an oppressor (ẓālim), or rather, a disbeliever (kāfir). «الا لیعبدون... للّذین ظلموا... كفروا»

2- The Divine tradition regarding oppressors is consistent throughout history. «ذَنوباً مثل ذَنوب اصحابهم»

3- The history of past generations is the best evidence for the certainty of punishment for oppressors. «ذنوباً مثل ذنوب اصحابهم»

4- Similar actions result in similar punishments. «مثل ذنوب أصحابهم»

5- Similar behaviors link individuals together. «مثل ذنوب أصحابهم»

6- Allah's wrath and grace have a wise timing; our haste and impatience have no effect on it. «فلایستعجلون»

7- Man's destiny is tied to his deeds. (Oppression and disbelief are the cause of Divine punishment.) «فویل للّذین كفروا...»

8- Foresight prevents man from disbelief and oppression. «فویل للّذین كفروا من یومهم الّذى یوعدون»
